title: 2025 09 08 Logging Health Data Naviwell
html:
TL;DR

  • Step-by-step SOP Yahir wrote for himself running the NaviWell health-coaching workflow inside eClinicalWorks (ECW).
  • Patient onboarding requires email verification + a primary diagnosis — without the diagnosis, "nothing will populate."
  • Per-session vitals: resting heart rate + blood pressure every visit; weight + body fat once per month so insurance can see progress.
  • ECW charting recipe: open appointment, go to Hub, set Progress Notes template to "Trainer," assessment code Z71.82, CPT 99404, reason "Health Coaching HC."
  • Two patient names noted at the bottom: anontion acosta (VRML training) and jorge amaya (in-person 8:30am Monday).

Notes

This is operational, not philosophical — Yahir documenting the NaviWell + ECW charting flow so he doesn't have to re-derive it each session. Worth ingesting because it's the most concrete artifact of his actual day-job role at NaviWell: not just "wellness coach" in the abstract but the specific clicks (Hub → Progress Notes → Template Trainer → orange-text editing → Assessment Z71.82 → CPT 99404 → New Action "Close IG – [Patient]").

A few things stand out as Yahir's voice creeping into a procedural doc: the all-caps emphasis ("OR ELSE NOTHING WILL POPULATE"), the patient-behavior shortcut ("if they take it at home they 9/10 times will not take it"), and the funnel-down ("be pointing people down to the diets and recipes tab"). The note doubles as a bridge income artifact — the day-job that funds the agency/Rankett and Outpace work.

Connections

  • naviwell group — employer; SOP for their core workflow.
  • ecw — the EHR Yahir charts inside; first detailed entry.
  • bridge income strategies — NaviWell is Yahir's bridge income while building.
  • anontion acosta / jorge amaya — patients named in scheduling notes.
